Off Calendar May-09-2022 Continued To Jul-12-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar May-23-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT AIRBNB, INC.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration.

APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Jamie Goldstein, Esq. (510.629.4877; jamie@aswtlawyers.com) of Arias, Sanguinetti, Wang & Torrijos, LLP for Plaintiff.
Maxwell Schjrieffer, Esq. (626.373.2444; mrs@pksllp.com) of P.K. Schrieffer LLP for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant AIRBNB, INC.'s motion to compel arbitration and stay is denied.

Sec. 21.1 of the parties' agreement provides: "If your Country of Residence is the United States, these Terms will be interpreted in accordance with the laws of the State of California and the United States of America, without regard to conflict-of-law provisions." (Cadambi Decl., Ex. F.) Based on this language, plaintiff can seek to avoid arbitration based on CCP 1281.2(c) [potential for conflicting rulings]. (See Mount Diablo Medical Center v. Health Net of California, Inc. (2002) 101 Cal.App.4th 711, 723-726 ["generic" California choice of law provision which does not mention arbitration but is "broad, unqualified and all encompassing" evidence that the parties intended Section 1281.2 to apply to arbitration agreement]; Cronus Investments, Inc. v. Concierge Services (2005) 35 Cal.4th 376, 388-93.)

The CCP 1281.2(c) issue is directed to the delegation clause itself so the court will rule on the issue. "[I]f the party is making a specific challenge to the delegation clause, the court must determine whether the delegation clause itself may be enforced (and can only delegate the general issue of enforceability to the arbitrator if it first determines the delegation clause is enforceable)." (Malone v. Superior Court (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 1551, 1560.)

Here, there is a potential for conflicting rulings because the owner of the property (Fileri) is being sued in this case and he is not obligated to arbitrate his dispute with plaintiff. The court therefore denies the motion to compel arbitration and a stay.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Vienna Nguyen, CSR# 13137 (949.295.5737; viennanguyen@gmail.com), Reported. =(302/RBU)
